publisherName: City of Duluth Lead Removal Program
claimSummary: Duluth says its service line inventory is publicly available, shows lead, galvanized, unknown, and non-lead counts, and directs residents to the Lead Inventory Tracking Tool or city contact for address checks.
reviewerNote: Official Duluth inventory page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Duluth Lead Removal Program
claimSummary: Duluth says owners in planned replacement areas are notified in advance by mail, email, phone calls, and door knocks before construction begins.
reviewerNote: Official Duluth replacement page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Duluth Lead Removal Program
claimSummary: Duluth says federal and state funding make it possible to conduct lead service line replacements at no cost to property owners through city-planned projects.
reviewerNote: Official Duluth replacement program page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Duluth Lead Removal Program
claimSummary: Duluth says project-area lead service line replacements are funded at no cost to property owners and reimbursement outside the city program is not allowed with this funding.
reviewerNote: Official Duluth no-cost replacement language reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Hamilton Utilities + Public Works
claimSummary: Hamilton says its interactive lead service line map provides the best available data for both customer-owned and utility-owned portions of the service line.
reviewerNote: Official Hamilton lead awareness page reviewed manually for map and ownership-split language.

publisherName: City of Hamilton Utilities + Public Works
claimSummary: Hamilton explains flush and filter guidance, says private-side replacement cost remains the owner's responsibility, and says the city replaces known city-owned lead service lines during water main projects.
reviewerNote: Official Hamilton public guidance reviewed manually for owner responsibility and water-main-project replacement rules.

claimSummary: Phoenix says its service line materials inventory is publicly available, includes non-lead lead galvanized and unknown categories, and customers can use the map to view their service line material.
reviewerNote: Official Phoenix inventory page reviewed manually for current material categories.

claimSummary: Phoenix says customers with lead galvanized or unknown service lines were sent mail notifications as replacement work began in fall 2024, and that unknown lines are unlikely to be lead but still receive second verification and next-step notices.
reviewerNote: Official Phoenix notification guidance reviewed manually for mailed notice timing and unknown-line follow-up wording.

claimSummary: Phoenix says when a galvanized or lead service line is identified, the city schedules replacement, replaces the full water service line from the meter box to the house connection, and covers the entire cost.
reviewerNote: Official Phoenix replacement plan page reviewed manually for full-line replacement scope and full cost coverage.

claimSummary: Phoenix says the city covers the entire cost of the replacement process, including full water service line replacement and disturbed-area restoration, when replacement is required.
reviewerNote: Official Phoenix replacement cost language reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Tempe says customers can review the public inventory, search an address, complete the survey, and that the city has no records of any known public lead service lines.
reviewerNote: Official Tempe service line inventory page reviewed manually for survey workflow and no-known-public-lead language.

claimSummary: Tempe says unknown notices are precautionary while survey and field verification continue, customers can search the public inventory, and if any lead or galvanized line is identified the city will notify customers and work with them on replacement and funding options.
reviewerNote: Official Tempe lead and copper notice page reviewed manually for precautionary notice and follow-up language.

claimSummary: Tucson Water says customers can view the service line inventory online and that the utility is conducting citywide inspections to update unknown and galvanized records.
reviewerNote: Official Tucson inventory page reviewed manually for online inventory and citywide inspection language.

claimSummary: Tucson Water says annual notices explain unknown and galvanized statuses, those notices do not by themselves mean a line contains lead, and the utility will reach out if additional steps or replacement are needed.
reviewerNote: Official Tucson notification guidance reviewed manually for annual notice and no-immediate-action language.

claimSummary: Tucson Water says if inspections confirm a service line requires replacement, the utility will reach out with next steps and complete the work at no cost to the customer.
reviewerNote: Official Tucson replacement program page reviewed manually for confirmed-replacement workflow.

claimSummary: Tucson Water says confirmed service line replacements are completed at no cost to the customer once inspection determines replacement is required.
reviewerNote: Official Tucson replacement cost language reviewed manually for no-cost confirmed replacement language.
